reforms.description: The government has taken steps to tackle the shortage of technicians through the adoption of a Technology Pact, aimed at increasing the share of graduates with science or engineering degrees.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing the number of graduates in science and engineering, the reform aims to enhance the skilled workforce available for R&D and innovation activities, thereby supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has taken welcome steps to tackle the shortage of technicians with the adoption of a Technology Pact.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative addresses a critical skills gap in the workforce, which is essential for supporting innovation and R&D activities in the economy.
reforms.source_quote: More recently, the government has taken welcome steps to tackle the shortage of technicians with the adoption of a Technology Pact.

reforms.description: The government has considered converting the two-rate corporate income tax into a single rate tax system while not increasing the tax burden on SMEs, aiming to level the playing field between smaller and bigger companies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: A single tax rate could reduce disincentives for SMEs to grow and encourage investment, thereby enhancing overall economic dynamism.
reforms.status_evidence: The government had considered this reform but it has not yet been implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant change in tax policy that could impact the growth of SMEs, although it is not a complete overhaul of the tax system.
reforms.source_quote: Consider converting the two-rate corporate income tax into a single rate tax system while not increasing the tax burden on SMEs.

reforms.description: The government plans to ease access to licenses by issuing them automatically if they are not delivered by the end of the statutory response period, aiming to reduce administrative burdens for SMEs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By simplifying the licensing process, the reform is expected to lower barriers to entrepreneurship, facilitating the establishment and growth of new businesses.
reforms.status_evidence: Plans to ease access to licenses have been mentioned as part of the recommendations.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant in its potential to streamline processes for SMEs, although it does not represent a comprehensive reform of the regulatory framework.
reforms.source_quote: Ease access to licences by issuing them automatically if they are not delivered by the end of the statutory response period.

reforms.description: The SME+ Innovation Fund was introduced to support small and medium-sized enterprises in their innovation activities, aiming to enhance their competitiveness and growth potential.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing financial support for innovation, the fund aims to increase the capacity of SMEs to develop new products and services, thereby enhancing productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The SME+ Innovation Fund was mentioned as a new initiative to support SMEs.
reforms.importance_rationale: The fund represents a significant effort to bolster innovation among SMEs, which are crucial for economic dynamism, but it is not a comprehensive overhaul of the innovation system.
reforms.source_quote: The SME+ Innovation Fund was introduced to support small and medium-sized enterprises in their innovation activities.

reforms.description: The Netherlands plans to allow public research institutes to take equity stakes in young businesses, broadening access to academic research and increasing direct innovation grants to SMEs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This reform aims to enhance the financial support and collaboration between public research entities and startups, fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan to allow public research institutes to take equity stakes has been outlined as a recommendation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in how public research institutes can engage with the private sector, potentially leading to increased innovation and collaboration.
reforms.source_quote: Allow public research institutes to take equity stakes in young business, broaden access to academic research and increase the share of direct innovation grants to SMEs.

reforms.description: The Dutch government launched several programmes to ease access to finance for SMEs and start-ups, including higher guarantees for bank lending and the establishment of a microcredit institution, Qredits.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These measures aim to reduce credit constraints for innovative firms, thereby enhancing their ability to invest and grow, which supports overall economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has actively launched and supported these programmes since 2009.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant structural change in the financing landscape for SMEs, addressing critical market failures and enhancing access to capital for innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The government launched a number of programmes to ease access to finance during the crisis.

reforms.description: The Dutch government is considering setting up a National Mortgage Institute to securitise part of the mortgage debt, aiming to increase banks' funding stability and lower costs for borrowers.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing funding stability for banks, the initiative is expected to lower borrowing costs, thereby supporting economic activity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is considering setting up a National Mortgage Institute.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant policy shift aimed at stabilizing the banking sector and improving access to finance for households, which is crucial for economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The government is considering setting up a National Mortgage Institute, which would securitise part of the mortgage debt and sell it to international and national institutional investors.

reforms.description: The Dutch government expanded the SME loan guarantee scheme (BMKB) to increase the maximum loan amount and guarantee coverage, aimed at improving access to bank lending for small and medium-sized enterprises with limited collateral.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating access to finance, the scheme supports the growth and sustainability of SMEs, which are crucial for economic recovery and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The maximum loan amount and guarantee percentage were increased to support SMEs.
reforms.importance_rationale: The expansion of the BMKB scheme represents a significant adjustment to existing financial support mechanisms for SMEs, enhancing their ability to secure necessary funding.
reforms.source_quote: The maximum loan amount of the SME loan guarantee scheme was increased from EUR 1 million to EUR 1.5 million per enterprise.

reforms.description: The Dutch government launched the GO facility, a business loan guarantee scheme aimed at low-risk firms in the expansion or consolidation stage, providing banks with a 50% guarantee on new loans.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This scheme enhances access to finance for SMEs, which is essential for their growth and innovation potential, thereby supporting overall economic recovery.
reforms.status_evidence: The GO scheme provides banks with a 50% guarantee on new bank loans.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of the GO facility represents a significant effort to improve financing options for SMEs, particularly during economic downturns.
reforms.source_quote: In 2009, the government launched a business loan guarantee scheme targeted to low-risk firms in the expansion or consolidation stage of development.

reforms.description: The Dutch government launched the 'SME+ Innovation Fund' to support innovative and fast-growing SMEs by mobilizing private capital and providing direct 'Innovation Credits' for R&D projects.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The fund aims to enhance R&D investment in SMEs, thereby fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The fund has been operational since its launch in January 2012.
reforms.importance_rationale: The SME+ Innovation Fund represents a significant structural change in how the government supports innovation financing for SMEs, addressing a critical equity gap.
reforms.source_quote: In January 2012, the government launched the 'SME+ Innovation Fund'. The Fund is an umbrella for different (partly existing) innovation finance schemes.

reforms.description: The government announced an addition to the SME+ Innovation Fund, introducing an early stage instrument and a co-investment facility for business angels to support innovative start-ups.
reforms.component_name: Early Stage Instrument and Co-Investment Facility
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This initiative aims to leverage private investment in innovative start-ups, enhancing their growth potential and contribution to the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has announced the addition of an early stage instrument and a co-investment facility for business angels.
reforms.importance_rationale: This addition enhances the existing SME+ Innovation Fund, providing targeted support for early-stage innovative firms.
reforms.source_quote: Recently, the government has announced an addition to the SME+ Innovation Fund of an early stage instrument and a co-investment facility for business angels.

reforms.description: The Netherlands has launched a two-pillar approach to promote a healthy entrepreneurial system with innovation at its core, focusing on enhancing framework conditions for the business sector and developing sector-specific policies to boost R&D in nine 'top sectors'.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This approach aims to enhance R&D and innovation, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The two-pillar approach has been launched to promote innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a structural change in the innovation policy framework, aiming to significantly enhance R&D and innovation across key sectors.
reforms.source_quote: The Netherlands has launched an approach based on two complementary pillars to promote a healthy entrepreneurial system with innovation at its core.

reforms.description: The government has introduced knowledge and innovation contracts for 'top sectors', facilitating easier access for SMEs to innovation instruments through the SME innovation scheme.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving access to innovation instruments, this reform supports SME growth and innovation capacity.
reforms.status_evidence: The introduction of knowledge and innovation contracts has been established.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform enhances existing innovation support mechanisms, particularly for SMEs, but does not represent a complete overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: The recent creation of knowledge and innovation contracts for 'top sectors'... is a step forward.

reforms.description: The government has launched a rationalisation of its support network for businesses by merging local and national Chambers of Commerce with the Syntens Innovation Centre into a single organisation, aimed at promoting the development of SMEs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Streamlining support services is expected to enhance accessibility and efficiency for SMEs, fostering innovation and growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The local and national Chambers of Commerce were merged into a single organisation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the support system for SMEs, which is crucial for enhancing their growth and innovation capacity.
reforms.source_quote: The government has also launched a welcome rationalisation of its support network for businesses, which should further promote the development of SMEs.

reforms.description: The government aims to strengthen incentives for universities to commercialise their research by allowing them to take equity stakes in small businesses and encouraging free access to research and unexploited patents.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhances the link between academic research and market applications, fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has announced plans to strengthen incentives for universities.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in how universities engage with the private sector, potentially leading to increased innovation and economic activity.
reforms.source_quote: Strengthen incentives for universities to commercialise their research by allowing them to take equity stakes in small businesses.
